name: Sierra de Loarre – circular from Huesca
name_original: Sierra de Loarre – circular desde Huesca
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Hoya De Huesca traverse a diverse landscape, transitioning between the Pre-Pyrenean mountains and the Ebro valley in Spain's Aragon region. This area offers varied terrain, from flat plains to rolling hills and challenging mountain passes, suitable for different cycling experiences. The geography includes significant natural features like the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park and the striking Mallos de Riglos. Most routes feature paved surfaces, making them ideal for road bikes. Best road cycling routes around Hoya De Huesca The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Loarre – circular from Huesca, a 70.7 miles (113.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain and passes near the historic Loarre Castle. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Salto de Roldán Loop from Huesca, a moderate 36.1 miles (58.1 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the Salto de Roldán rock formations and surrounding natural beauty. Local road cyclists also love the Grañén, Tardienta, Almudévar, a 41.4 miles (66.6 km) trail leading through agricultural plains and villages, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Hoya De Huesca is defined by its mix of Pre-Pyrenean mountains, the Ebro valley, and prominent rock formations like the Mallos de Riglos. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Hoya De Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hoya De Huesca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Hoya de Huesca offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 90 tours available on komoot. This includes a diverse range of options, from easy rides to challenging mountain passes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The region provides a varied cycling experience, transitioning between the Pre-Pyrenean mountains and the Ebro valley. You'll find a mix of flat plains, rolling hills, and more demanding mountain passes. Most routes feature paved surfaces, making them ideal for road bikes.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Yes, there are easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930643180/alerre-y-cuarte-from-huesca-camino-natural-de-loreto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alerre y Cuarte from Huesca (Camino Natural de Loreto)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 25.6 km route with minimal elevation gain, offering a gentle introduction to road cycling in the area.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Hoya de Huesca has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919707139/sierra-de-loarre-circular-from-huesca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Loarre – circular from Huesca&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 113.8 km route with significant elevation gain, passing near the historic Loarre Castle. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926228562/la-sotonera-reservoir-loop-from-huesca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sotonera Reservoir Loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;, covering nearly 130 km with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the region?
A: Yes, many routes in Hoya de Huesca are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926091592/guara-y-hoya-de-huesca-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guara y Hoya de Huesca – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926179661/robres-lanaja-sarinena-circular-from-granen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robres, Lanaja, Sariñena – circular from Grañén&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery and distances.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Hoya de Huesca often pass by stunning natural features. You can encounter the impressive rock formations of the Mallos de Riglos and Mallos de Agüero, or cycle through areas near the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. Routes also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Escalete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several picturesque reservoirs that make for scenic cycling backdrops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926228562/la-sotonera-reservoir-loop-from-huesca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sotonera Reservoir Loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering extensive views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3562656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sotonera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while road cycling in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Yes, the varied landscape of Hoya de Huesca supports a rich ecosystem. Cyclists may spot diverse wildlife, including wild boar, roe deer, and various birds of prey such as griffon vultures and bearded vultures, especially in areas bordering natural parks like Sierra y Cañones de Guara. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/324002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riglos Griffon Vulture Viewing Area&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot for birdwatching.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The road cycling routes in Hoya de Huesca are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse terrain, stunning natural beauty, and the well-maintained paved surfaces that are ideal for road bikes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for families with older children?
A: While road cycling can be challenging for very young children, many moderate routes are suitable for families with older, experienced children. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926117732/granen-tardienta-almudevar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grañén, Tardienta, Almudévar&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through agricultural plains, offer a less strenuous option compared to mountain passes, making them more accessible for family outings.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Hoya de Huesca is generally favorable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for longer rides. Summer can be warm, especially in the plains, but early mornings or routes at higher elevations provide cooler conditions. Winters are milder than in the high Pyrenees, making cycling possible on many days.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that offer historical or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. Many routes pass by significant landmarks, such as the well-preserved Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919707139/sierra-de-loarre-circular-from-huesca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also cycle through charming villages with historical architecture, providing a blend of natural beauty and cultural discovery.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park?
A: The Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, renowned for its spectacular canyons and rock formations, is a prominent feature of the region. While some routes may skirt its edges or offer views into the park, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926091592/guara-y-hoya-de-huesca-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guara y Hoya de Huesca – circular route&lt;/a&gt; provides an excellent opportunity to experience the stunning scenery and unique geological features of this area by road bike.
tips:
This exceptional point of view also gives you access to the Colegida de Santa Maria la Mayor.

 Enter the church without hesitation to admire its magnificent altarpiece dedicated to the Virgin Mary.
View of the most famous "mallos" in the area, raised by the Alpine orogeny about 65 million years ago and subsequently eroded by water.
This bridge, near the mouth of the Garona river in the Gállego, parallel to a railway bridge (in the image) gives access to the special Garona valley.

A little more than 20 kilometers from Huesca, behind the Gratal and El Águila mountains, is Arguis. A picturesque town that recalls the Pyrenean villages for its stone houses topped by the typical scarecrows from their chimneys. It also has a beautiful Romanesque-style church dating from the 12th century.

Surrounded by a beautiful landscape, its reservoir appears, built at the beginning of the 18th century, it is a must for fans of fishing and hiking, since there are many routes that run through these places.

Next to the reservoir, in the old forest house, the Arguis Interpretation Center opens its doors for you to get to know in depth, and in a dynamic and interactive way, the peculiarities of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

More information: https://www.turismodearagon.com/ficha/arguis/
Inside the Monastery of San Pedro el Viejo there is an impressive cloister with round arches and double columns with well-preserved elaborate capitals.
